prompt: A surreal, uncanny image depicting Simpson's Paradox. A grand spiral staircase in an Escher-like interior where each individual flight of stairs clearly ascends upward, but the overall structure paradoxically descends. Each section of stairs is made of different colored marble — blue, green, red — each section goes up. But viewed as a whole, the staircase spirals downward into darkness. Dramatic chiaroscuro lighting. No text, no numbers, no charts.
visual_logic: Three color-coded flights of stairs each visibly ascend, but their combined spiral structure paradoxically descends — mirroring how subgroup trends reverse when aggregated.
